topic.name: Maritime Legislation and Environmental Protection
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various legislative measures related to maritime operations, environmental regulations, and the management of marine resources in U.S. waters.

Document 1: authorizes the U.S. Coast Guard to enter into certain agreements for the acquisition of heavy polar icebreakers
acquisition of offshore patrol cutters and fast response cutters
Document 2: amends the Hydrographic Services Improvement Act to authorize appropriations for hydrographic data
necessary to ensure safe navigation and improve management of coastal change in the Arctic
Document 3: establishment of national standards and prohibitions for discharges from cruise vessels
requires the Administrator of the EPA to establish effluent limits for sewage and graywater discharges
Document 4: extends the exemption of certain vessels from fire-retardant material requirements
requires owners to notify crew members about fire safety standards
Document 5: extends provisions prohibiting the EPA from requiring an NPDES permit for certain vessel discharges
covers discharges incidental to the normal operation of a covered vessel
Document 6: directs the Secretary of Commerce to urge international fishery management organizations to adopt shark conservation measures
prohibits removal of shark fins and carcass disposal at sea
Document 7: revises the amount and apportionment of recovery in admiralty civil actions
allows recovery for nonpecuniary loss due to wrongful acts on the high seas

topic.name: Federal Grant Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various federal grant programs aimed at supporting community development, conservation, education, and employment initiatives.

Document 1: establish a Connecticut River Grants and Technical Assistance Program
provide grants and technical assistance to the governments of New Hampshire and Vermont
Document 2: participation in the Small Community Air Service Development Program
criteria the Secretary of Transportation must apply
Document 3: authorize the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to make grants
fund the demolition of vacant housing
Document 4: establish the Youth Summer Employment Success Fund
provide summer employment opportunities youth aged 16-24
Document 5: award grants to educational or nonprofit nongovernmental entities
conduct activities in states adjacent to a Great Lake
Document 6: make grants to eligible entities to carry out an age-appropriate, research-based Internet safety education program
provide technical assistance to recipients of such grants
Document 7: award grants to community colleges
facilitate the establishment of an advisory board to provide guidance to the training program

topic.name: Oil and Gas Leasing Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the exploration, development, and regulation of oil and gas resources in the United States, particularly in offshore and protected areas.

Document 1: Revokes all existing withdrawals by the President under the authority of the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act.
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to set forth additional incentives for investment in oil refineries.
Document 2: Requires the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to conduct and report to Congress on an assessment of all available domestic technological capabilities required for the location and the efficient and environmentally sound recovery of minerals.
Defines 'shallow and deep seabed of the United States' as areas of the seabed contiguous to and within 200 miles of the territorial sea of the United States.
Document 3: Repeals the prohibition under the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2008 on the use of funds to prepare or publish final regulations regarding a commercial leasing program for oil shale.
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to issue all regulations necessary to implement the Oil Share, Tar Sands, and Other Strategic Unconventional Fuels Act of 2005.
Document 4: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from offering for oil and gas leasing or preleasing any tract located in specified areas of the Eastern Gulf of Mexico.
Prohibits the Secretary from offering for oil and gas leasing or preleasing any tract located in specified areas.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to implement a competitive leasing program for the exploration, development, and production of the oil and gas resources on the Coastal Plain of Alaska.

Amends the Alaska National Interest Lands Conservation Act of 1980 to repeal the prohibition against leasing or other development leading to production of oil and gas from the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge.
Document 6: Deems the Draft Proposed Outer Continental Shelf Oil and Gas Leasing Program 2010-2015 issued by the Secretary of the Interior to be approved as a final oil and gas leasing program.
Directs the Secretary to conduct a lease sale in each outer Continental Shelf (OCS) planning area for which there is a commercial interest in purchasing federal oil and gas production leases.
Document 7: Authorizes exploration, leasing, development, and production of oil and gas from the Western Coastal Plain.
Directs the Secretary to establish lease sale procedures and offer for lease those tracts the Secretary considers to have the greatest potential for the discovery of hydrocarbons.

topic.name: Naming Federal Facilities
topic.description: The documents discuss the designation and naming of various federal facilities across the United States.

Document 1: Designates the U.S. courthouse located at 2601 2nd Avenue North, Billings, Montana, as the 'James F. Battin United States Courthouse.'
Declares that the 'James F. Battin United States Courthouse' located at 315 North 26th Street, Billings, Montana, shall no longer be so named.
Document 2: Designates the federal building located at 210 Walnut Street in Des Moines, Iowa, as the 'Neal Smith Federal Building.'
Document 3: Designates the U.S. courthouse located at 501 East Court Street in Jackson, Mississippi, as the 'R. Jess Brown United States Courthouse.'
Document 4: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 9727 Antioch Road in Overland Park, Kansas, as the 'Congresswoman Jan Meyers Post Office Building.'
Document 5: Directs the President to appoint: (1) four additional permanent district judges for the eastern district of California; and (2) one temporary judgeship for such district.
Document 6: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 229 West Main Cross Street, in Findlay, Ohio, as the 'Michael Garver Oxley Memorial Post Office Building.'
Document 7: Designates the facility of the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) at 9800 West Commercial Boulevard in Sunrise, Florida, as the 'William 'Bill' Kling VA Clinic.'

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Actions on Security and Compensation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative measures related to national security, compensation for victims of terrorism, and regulatory actions affecting various sectors.

Document 1: Authorizes the President to compensate a U.S. national...
Limits an award to $500,000, adjusted to reflect the annual change in the Consumer Price Index.
Document 2: Amends the Sherman Act to make it illegal for any foreign state...
Denies a foreign state engaged in such conduct sovereign immunity from the jurisdiction or judgments of U.S. courts.
Document 4: Prohibits a national security letter from being issued unless...
Provides a civil cause of action for the misuse of letters.
Document 5: Prohibits a state, local government, or any officer...
Establishes a fine of up to $5,000 a day on any state or local government...
Document 7: Amends the USA PATRIOT Improvement and Reauthorization Act of 2005 to extend...
Authorizing the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) to apply for a court order requiring production of tangible things...

topic.name: Medicare and Health Policy
topic.description: Legislation related to Medicare services, coverage, and healthcare provider regulations.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to revise the definition of 'federally qualified health center services'
Permits payment for hospital-based services.
Document 2: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act (SSA) to require state Medicaid plans to identify prescription drugs that present a high-risk of misuse
Requires a state to establish a Medicaid Lock-in Program.
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to repeal the allowance for disenrollment
Restores the option under previous law to elect to change from a MedicareAdvantage to the original Medicare fee-for-service plan.
Document 4: Declares the sense of Congress that colorectal cancer screening tests should be included as part of any basic benefit package
Amends SSA title XVIII (Medicare) to eliminate coinsurance for colorectal cancer screening tests.
Document 5: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to increase the Medicare caps on the total number of full-time equivalent residents
Requires the aggregate number of such increases for a state to be at least 15.
Document 6: Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Benefits) of the Social Security Act to extend the months of coverage of immunosuppressive drugs for kidney transplant patients
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to determine a monthly premium rate for such individuals.
Document 7: This bill amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to redistribute unused residency positions
The bill establishes specified priorities, limitations, and capacity considerations with respect to redistribution.

topic.name: Social Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various amendments and acts related to the Social Security system, focusing on benefits, eligibility, and adjustments for different demographics.

Document 1: Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) (OASDI) of the Social Security Act to revise the formula for the computation of minimum Old Age Insurance benefits.
Sets forth a schedule of additional benefit increases for such beneficiaries.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code (IRC) and title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) (OASDI) of the Social Security Act (SSA) to establish a Social Security Guarantee Program.
Repeal the limitation on the amount of outside income which beneficiaries age 62 or over may earn.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code and the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA) to treat employee benefit or pension plans maintained by Indian tribes in the same manner as plans maintained by states.
Document 4: Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) (OASDI) of the Social Security Act to tie the CPIEC to computation of cost-of-living increases in OASDI benefits.
Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to disburse a cash payment of $250 for each non-COLA year to certain recipients of OASDI benefits.
Document 5: Amends the Social Security Act to require each state to implement a reemployment adjustment assistance program.
Establishes a Wage Insurance Trust Fund in the Treasury.
Document 6: Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) (OASDI) of the Social Security Act to exclude from creditable wages any wages earned for services by aliens not authorized to be employed.
Self-employment income derived from a trade or business conducted in the United States, while the alien was not authorized to be so employed.
Document 7: Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) (OASDI) of the Social Security Act to authorize the treatment of permanent partnerships between individuals of the same gender as marriage for purposes of determining entitlement to OASDI benefits.

topic.name: Higher Education Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative amendments and acts related to higher education, student loans, and institutional accountability.

Document 1: requires the Department of Education's Office of Civil Rights to publish the names of institutions of higher education that request and receive religious exemptions from title IX
amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965
Document 3: amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to increase the amount of the outstanding principal and interest on capital financing bonds for historically Black colleges and universities
Document 4: amends the federal bankruptcy code to eliminate the provision that makes certain education debts non-dischargeable in bankruptcy
permits a borrower to discharge in bankruptcy a nonprofit, government, or private student loan
Document 5: amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to cancel 15%, 15%, 20%, 20%, and 30% of the amount a borrower owes after 2, 4, 6, 8, and 10 years of public service employment
places the borrower's loan in deferment during the period in which the borrower is employed in an eligible public service job
Document 7: amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require proprietary institutions of higher education to derive not less than 10% of their revenue from sources other than title IV
requires these schools to derive not less than 10% of their revenue from sources other than title IV or become ineligible for title IV funding

topic.name: Aviation Safety Regulations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing safety standards and operational protocols in the aviation industry.

Document 1: Minimum Staffing of Air Traffic Controllers Act of 2015
ensures that a commercial airport has at least two air traffic controllers on duty
Document 2: Safe Egress in Air Travel Act of 2016
establish minimum dimensions for passenger seats
Document 3: No Armed Drones Act of 2012
prohibit the operation of drones as weapons in national airspace
Document 4: Airplane Kids In Transit Safety Act of 2016
require aircraft medical kits to include emergency medications for children
Document 5: Safe Skies Act of 2012
modify flightcrew member duty and rest requirements for all-cargo flightcrew members
Document 6: Airport and Airway Extension Act of 2011
extend authority for airport improvement projects
Document 7: Safe Aviation and Flight Emergency Tracking Act of 2014
ensure continuous tracking of aircraft during flight

topic.name: Veterans and Vulnerable Populations Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at providing support and protection for veterans and vulnerable populations, including those affected by substance abuse, elder abuse, and child exploitation.

Document 1: Requires the Director of the Office of National Drug Control Policy to delegate to the Department of Justice (DOJ) the authority to administer the veteran's treatment courts program established by this Act.
Programs that involve continuing judicial supervision over nonviolent offenders with substance abuse or mental health problems who have served in the U.S. military.
Document 2: Directs the Department of Justice's (DOJ's) Bureau of Justice Assistance (BJA) to award grants to state and local law enforcement or public safety agencies to develop tracking technology programs to locate missing individuals with dementia or children with developmental disabilities.
Document 3: Directs the Attorney General to study and report to Congress on state laws and practices relating to elder abuse, neglect, and exploitation.
Authorize appropriations for FY2009-FY2015 for victim advocacy grants for study of the special needs of victims of elder abuse, neglect, and exploitation.
Document 4: Amends the Indian Civil Rights Act of 1968 to revise provisions regarding tribal jurisdiction over crimes of domestic violence, including to expand tribal criminal jurisdiction to include sex trafficking, sexual violence, stalking, and related conduct.
Document 6: Requires such service providers to preserve images of child pornography for evidentiary purposes.
Imposes enhanced criminal penalties for use of the Internet to violate child pornography or sexual exploitation laws.
Document 7: Amends the Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act to condition eligibility to receive a state grant for child abuse or neglect prevention and treatment programs on inclusion in the state plan of a certification by the governor that the state has in effect and is enforcing a state law.

topic.name: Clean Energy Policy and Regulation
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at promoting clean energy, electric vehicles, and renewable fuel standards in the United States.

Document 1: Establish standards for electric utilities regarding electric vehicle infrastructure.
Develop a plan to support the use of plug-in hybrid electric vehicles and electric vehicles.
Document 2: Provide tax incentives for energy conservation and production.
Allow tax credits for new alternative motor vehicles, including qualified plug-in electric drive motor vehicles.
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to include the Secretaries of Agriculture and Energy in renewable fuel program activities.
Require the Secretary of Energy to periodically update the baseline lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions.
Document 4: Repeal the renewable fuel standard.
Amends the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007.
Document 5: Prohibit the EPA from requiring transportation fuel to contain applicable volume of renewable fuel after 2022.
Waive applicable volume requirement for renewable fuel.
Document 6: Establish a Clean Energy Technology Manufacturing and Export Assistance Fund.
Promote policies that will reduce production costs and encourage innovation in the clean energy technology sector.
Document 7: Revise the renewable fuel standard program to require advanced biofuel.
Decrease the total volume of renewable fuel that must be contained in gasoline.

topic.name: Voting and Labor Rights Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing voting rights and protections for workers, particularly in the context of absentee voting and labor organization rights.

Document 1: Amends the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act to require states to accept and process absentee ballots of overseas uniformed services and civilian voters
requires the Presidential designee to reimburse overseas uniformed services voters for the costs of using a provider of express mail services
Document 2: Amends the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970 to provide for coverage under that Act of state and local government workers
Document 3: Amends the National Labor Relations Act and the Railway Labor Act to repeal those provisions that permit employers to require employees to join a union as a condition of employment
Document 4: Requires a labor organization to provide all employees covered by a collective bargaining agreement with written notice explaining how it calculated the share of such dues or fees related to non-political costs of collective bargaining
Prohibits a labor organization from collecting dues or fees from non-union employees without their consent
Document 5: Directs the Election Assistance Commission to establish a Vote by Mail program of grants to participating states
Directs the Comptroller General to evaluate and report to Congress on the benefits of broader implementation of mail-in voting in elections
Document 6: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board to certify a bargaining representative without directing an election if a majority of the bargaining unit employees have authorized designation of the representative
Adds to remedies for such violations: back pay plus liquidated damages
Document 7: Amends the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977 to require the Secretary of Labor to determine why an accident occurred and whether there were violations of law
Requires an independent accident investigation by an independent Panel appointed by the Secretary of Health and Human Services

topic.name: Veterans and Community Health Care
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving health care access and affordability for veterans, individuals with mental health needs, and the aging population.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of Veterans Affairs from collecting a copayment from a veteran being furnished inpatient or outpatient hospice care by the Department of Veterans Affairs.
Document 2: Eliminates part D cost-sharing for certain non-institutionalized full-benefit dual eligible individuals with income below 135% of the poverty line.
Document 3: Specifies that receiving hospital care or medical services under a medical care program of the Indian Health Service does not disqualify an individual from being eligible for a health savings account.
Document 4: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants for services to individuals with mental illnesses.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to reimburse a covered beneficiary under TRICARE for travel expenses.
Document 6: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to reimburse health professionals for continuing professional education expenses.
Document 7: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a Geriatric and Gerontology Loan Repayment Program.

topic.name: Energy Regulation and Security
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to the regulation, management, and security of energy resources, including natural gas and nuclear materials.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Power Act and the Natural Gas Act to direct the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to hold at least one public hearing in each affected county and locality before issuing any authorization for action that may affect public or private land use.
Requires FERC to hold one additional public hearing after the initial one if the government of such county or locality requests it in order to address an issue not addressed at the initial hearing.
Document 2: This bill amends the USEC Privatization Act governing uranium transfers and sales to require the Department of Energy (DOE) to issue, beginning January 1, 2017, and at least once every 10 years afterwards, a long-term excess uranium inventory management plan.
DOE may provide from its stockpile up to 2100 and up to 2700 metric tons of uranium in any form for the periods calendar 2016-2023 and beginning January 1, 2024, respectively.
Document 3: Directs the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) to issue a final rule requiring all commercial nuclear power reactors approved for construction after enactment of this Act to be designed to withstand a large commercial aircraft impact.
Requires the NRC to consider the likely consequences of a potential terrorist attack in any review it is required to undertake under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969.
Document 4: Amends federal transportation law to require the Surface Transportation Board to give greater weight to local official statements and public comments in opposition to the proposed construction.
Prescribes procedural requirements for dealing with any false or incorrect information provided by an applicant for a certificate for construction.
Document 5: Amends the Natural Gas Act to direct the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to make a public interest determination and issue an order for an application for the exportation of natural gas to a foreign country.
Requires the Court to set any civil action brought under this Act on the docket, for expedited consideration, as soon as practical after the filing date of the initial pleading.
Document 6: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to permit the use of rights of way for natural gas pipelines through public lands, forest, and other reservations of the United States.

Deems the issuance of a sundry notice or right-of-way for a gas gathering line and associated field compression unit located on federal or Indian land and servicing an oil well to be an action categorically excluded for purposes of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969.
Document 7: Establishes the Nuclear Waste Administration (NWA) to provide for the permanent disposal of nuclear waste, including the siting, construction, and operation of additional repositories.
Requires the Secretary to arrange for the Administrator to dispose of defense wastes.

topic.name: Federal Regulation of Substance and Firearm Use
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation of firearms, controlled substances, and associated penalties or requirements for compliance with federal laws.

Document 1: Prohibits the use of federal funds to require a person licensed to deal in firearms to report information to the Department of Justice regarding the sale of multiple rifles or shotguns to the same person.
Document 2: Requires the Attorney General's prior written approval for a federal prosecution of an offense under the Controlled Substances Act or the Controlled Substances Import and Export Act.
Document 3: Amends the REAL ID Act to prohibit a state from issuing a driver's license or identification document unless certain citizenship verification requirements are satisfied.
Document 4: Directs the Department of Transportation to study motor vehicle refueling assistance to qualified individuals with disabilities.
Document 5: Amends the Controlled Substances Import and Export Act to impose a fine and/or 10-year prison term on anyone who knowingly or intentionally traffics in a controlled substance within a federal or state Indian reservation.
Document 6: Amends the NICS Improvement Amendments Act to increase the amount of a state's allocation under the Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant Program that the Attorney General shall withhold.
Document 7: Allows a student convicted of a drug offense involving marijuana possession to resume or retain federal student aid eligibility while completing a drug rehabilitation program.

topic.name: Privacy Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures aimed at protecting personal privacy and sensitive information.

Document 1: Intimate Privacy Protection Act of 2016
makes it unlawful to knowingly distribute a photograph, film, or video of a person engaging in sexually explicit conduct without consent.
Document 4: Prohibits any financial or related institution from soliciting a Social Security number from an individual unless for specific uses.
Prohibits a covered entity from displaying an individual's Social Security account number on any card commonly provided to employees.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at providing tax credits and incentives for businesses, health care, education, and renewable energy.

Document 1: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to the work opportunity tax credit
increase the rate of such credit to 50% for members of a targeted group
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow taxpayers an election to receive a direct payment for investing in or producing specified energy property
extend eligibility for such payments until January 1, 2013
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow certain small business owners a refundable tax credit for health care expenses
for the payment of a portion of the health care expenses of their employees
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the 15-year recovery period classification for qualified leasehold improvement
for purposes of the depreciation tax deduction
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from the gross income of an employee amounts paid by an employer to a qualified college savings assistance plan
for a designated beneficiary
Document 6: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2018 the income tax credit for biodiesel and renewable diesel
the excise tax credit for biodiesel mixtures
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to impose an excise tax on payments made pursuant to a U.S. government contract
tax revenues raised by this Act should be used for the financing of a Fair Elections Fund

topic.name: Flood and Water Management Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on flood mitigation, water quality improvement, and ecosystem restoration in various regions of the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes grants to local governments for flood mitigation projects.
Projects with significant capacities for job creation are prioritized.
Document 2: Modifies flood damage reduction projects in California.
Authorizes improvements to flood damage reduction facilities.
Document 3: Amends the Clean Water Act to include programs for beach water pollution prevention.
Requires rapid testing methods and communication measures for water quality.
Document 4: Directs feasibility studies for watershed restoration and flood control in California.
Identifies demonstration projects for ecosystem restoration.
Document 5: Amends the Clean Water Act for Chesapeake Bay restoration monitoring.
Requires states to report on nutrient and sediment reduction goals.
Document 6: Declares navigational servitude exemptions for Texas City Ship Channel.
Document 7: Exempts specific dam projects from Federal Energy Regulatory Commission licensing.

topic.name: U.S. Trade and Sanctions Policy
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures related to trade regulations, countervailing duties, and sanctions against foreign entities, particularly focusing on Iran and currency misalignment issues.

Document 1: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to expand the authority of the administering authority or the International Trade Commission (ITC) to impose countervailing duties
Includes exchange-rate misalignment (undervaluation of a foreign currency) by a country as a countervailable subsidy.
Document 2: requires the President to block and prohibit all transactions in property and property interests of a foreign person that knowingly supports or conducts certain transactions with Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps
The President shall review an identified foreign person to determine whether there is sufficient evidence to impose sanctions.
Document 3: Amends the Caribbean Basin Economic Recovery Act to make permanent the extension of temporary duty-free treatment of certain textile and apparel articles manufactured in a beneficiary country
imported into the United States.
Document 4: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to modify the residence and income source rules involving U.S. possessions
The bill specifies that a bona fide resident of a possession is a person who has a substantial presence in the possession for at least 122 days during the calendar year.
Document 5: prescribes requirements for mandatory sanctions with respect to Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC)
The Department of the Treasury shall establish and publish in the Federal Register an IRGC watch list.
Document 6: it is the policy of the United States to support the decision of state and local governments and educational institutions to divest from, and to prohibit the investment of assets they control in, persons that have investments of $20 million or more in Iran's energy sector

Amends the Investment Company Act of 1940 to shield any registered investment company from civil, criminal, or administrative action based upon its divesting from, or avoiding investing in, securities issued by such persons.
Document 7: requires the President to impose sanctions pursuant to the International Emergency Economic Powers Act

against a person that continues to knowingly and directly provide specialized financial messaging services to, or to knowingly enable or facilitate access to such messaging services for: the Central Bank of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea.

topic.name: Advanced Manufacturing and Technology Development
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at enhancing U.S. manufacturing capabilities and integrating advanced technologies into various sectors.

Document 1: Authorizes the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) to establish a program to designate up to 25 institutions of higher education as U.S. manufacturing universities

Requires an institution applying for such program to submit a plan describing its engineering programs, its relationship to manufacturing industries, and its ability to positively impact local and regional economic development.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to identify and encourage the use of advanced technologies, including 3-dimensional modeling and simulation software
Authorizes the Secretary to develop and implement incentives to accelerate the use of such technologies on such projects.
Document 3: Establishes in the Department of Commerce the Weather Mitigation Advisory and Research Board to promote and fund research and development (R&D)
Directs the Board to use amounts in the Fund to provide for R&D with respect to weather mitigations by grants to, or contracts or cooperative arrangements with, public or private agencies.
Document 4: Defines 'exascale computing' as computing through the use of a computing machine that performs near or above 10 to the 18th power floating point operations per second
Directs the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to conduct a research program to develop exascale computing machine architectures to promote DOE missions.
Document 5: DOE shall develop advanced cybersecurity applications and technologies for the energy sector
DOE must develop an advanced energy security program that secures diverse energy networks in order to increase the functional preservation of the electric grid operations.
Document 6: Directs the President to establish a National Integrated Coastal and Ocean Observation System to support national defense, marine commerce, navigation safety, weather, climate, and marine forecasting
Requires the National Ocean Research Leadership Council to serve as the System's policy and coordination oversight body.
Document 7: Directs the Committee on Technology to develop a national manufacturing competitiveness strategic plan to improve government coordination
Specifies the goals of the plan to be to promote growth, job creation, sustainability, and competitiveness in the U.S. manufacturing sector.

topic.name: Humanitarian Assistance and Rights
topic.description: The documents focus on U.S. legislation aimed at providing humanitarian assistance and promoting human rights for vulnerable populations affected by conflict and displacement.

Document 1: Permits religious symbols recognizing the religious background of members of the Armed Forces to be included as part of military memorials.
Addresses the recognition of diverse backgrounds in military memorials.
Document 2: Declares that foreign nongovernmental organizations shall not be ineligible for assistance under the Foreign Assistance Act based on health or medical services.
Supports international advocacy and lobbying activities for health services.
Document 3: Expresses the sense of Congress concerning Iraqi refugees and internally displaced persons.
Directs the Secretary of State to provide humanitarian assistance to vulnerable populations in Iraq.
Document 4: Requires that U.S. activities in South Sudan support the human rights of women.
Focuses on the political, social, and economic participation of women in post-conflict settings.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of State to develop a comprehensive regional strategy to address the mass displacement of Iraqis.
Includes plans to assess the strategy's impact on vulnerable Iraqis.
Document 6: Directs the President to report on post-earthquake humanitarian efforts in Haiti.
Includes efforts to prevent the spread of cholera and treat infected persons.
Document 7: Authorizes the establishment of a United States-Poland Parliamentary Youth Exchange Program.
Focuses on fostering international cooperation and understanding through youth exchange.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating and providing support for farming practices, subsidies, and disaster relief in the agricultural sector.

Document 1: amends the Agricultural Act of 2014
eliminates the existing exemption for family farms
Document 2: repeals a provision of the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008
establishing an inspection and grading program for catfish
Document 3: authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to make grants
to facilitate the ability of local farmers to grow food crops
Document 4: directs the Secretary of Agriculture to provide payments
for qualifying livestock disaster losses
Document 5: amends the Food and Nutrition Act of 2009
to increase the FY2010 amount for emergency food assistance
Document 6: amends the Food Security Act of 1985
to reduce maximum annual direct and counter-cyclical commodity payments
Document 7: amends the Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002
to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to make state grants for extension, education

topic.name: Federal Recognition and Compensation for Tribes
topic.description: This topic encompasses acts and amendments related to the federal recognition of Native American tribes and the compensation for damages to their lands or resources.

Document 1: Lumbee Recognition Act - Extends federal recognition to the Lumbee Tribe of North Carolina.
Makes the Tribe and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided to Indians.
Document 2: Amends the Lower Brule Sioux Tribe Infrastructure Development Trust Fund Act to increase the aggregate amount to be deposited into the Lower Brule Sioux Tribe Infrastructure Development Trust Fund.
States that this Act shall be considered to be full and final compensation to the Lower Brule Sioux Tribe and the Crow Creek Sioux Tribe.
Document 3: Establishes in the Treasury the Spokane Tribe of Indians Settlement Fund.
Requires the payment of compensation to the Spokane Business Council for the use of tribal lands.
Document 4: Amends the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act of 1996 to extend housing assistance for Native Americans.
Makes the Department of Hawaiian Home Lands of the state of Hawaii eligible for loan guarantees.
Document 5: Extends federal recognition to the Muscogee Nation of Florida.
Makes the Nation and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 6: Amends the Omnibus Indian Advancement Act to repeal the declaration that specified land accepted by the Secretary of the Interior for the benefit of the Lytton Rancheria in California shall be deemed to have been held in trust.
Allows the Lytton Rancheria to conduct activities for class II gaming on the land taken into trust.
Document 7: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to take it into trust for the benefit of the Tribe.
Designates Yuma county, Arizona, as the Tribe's service area for the delivery of federal services to enrolled Tribe members.

topic.name: U.S. Nuclear and Defense Policy
topic.description: Documents related to U.S. nuclear arms control, missile defense, and international security agreements.

Document 1: Prohibits the President from unilaterally reducing U.S. deployed or non-deployed nuclear weapons below New START Treaty levels
States that such provision shall not be construed to limit any action required by the New START Treaty
Document 2: Amends the Iran Sanctions Act of 1996 to provide that such Act shall cease to be effective on the date the President certifies to Congress that the International Atomic Energy Agency has reached a broader conclusion that all nuclear material in Iran remains in peaceful activities.
Document 3: Prohibits Department of Defense (DOD) funds for FY2012-FY2013 from being used to provide the Russian Federation with access to U.S. missile defense technology

Expresses the sense of Congress that an agreement regarding missile defense cooperation...shall not be legally or politically binding unless it is either specifically approved with the advice and consent of the Senate.
Document 4: Prohibits obligating or expending funds for the implementation of any agreement with the government of Iraq unless the agreement is in the form of a treaty
Expresses the sense of the Congress that full Iraqi sovereignty is in the U.S. national interest.
Document 5: Expresses the sense of Congress that the Donbass People's Militia has met the criteria for designation as a foreign terrorist organization
Directs the President to subject to sanctions any person in the United States who knowingly provides material support or resources to the Donbass People's Militia.
Document 6: Directs the Legal Advisor to the Secretary of State to submit to Congress an unclassified report providing the justification for the President's decision to deny Congress its constitutionally protected role
Prohibits funds from being authorized or appropriated to carry out any bilateral agreement...that is not a treaty approved by two-thirds of the Senate.
Document 7: Amends the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 to require a joint congressional resolution of approval for a subsequent arrangement to an agreement for peaceful nuclear cooperation
Directs the President to keep the House Committee on Foreign Affairs and the Senate Committee on Foreign Relations fully informed of any initiative or negotiations.

topic.name: Regulatory Impact on Small Entities
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving regulatory processes and their economic implications for small entities, particularly in relation to fiscal management and stakeholder involvement.

Document 1: Amends the Regulatory Flexibility Act of 1980 to revise the definition of 'rule' and define 'economic impact' with respect to small entities.
Requires initial and final regulatory flexibility analyses to describe alternatives to a proposed rule that minimize adverse economic impacts on small entities.
Document 4: Sets forth general requirements for the federal regulatory system, including the protection of public health and welfare and the promotion of predictability in the regulatory process.
Requires federal agencies to propose regulations only upon a reasoned determination that the benefits justify their costs.
Document 6: Authorizes the Chief Counsel of the Small Business Administration to request a review of federal agency certifications regarding significant economic impacts on small entities.
If determined that a proposed rule will have a significant economic impact, the federal agency must perform regulatory flexibility analyses.

topic.name: Tax Legislation and Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at reforming tax laws, enhancing taxpayer rights, and regulating tax preparation practices.

Document 1: Amends the Consumer Financial Protection Act of 2010 to require the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) to regulate refund anticipation payment arrangements.
Empowers the CFPB to take enforcement action against a preparer for specified violations.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to modify income tax forms to incorporate information currently required on the separate earned income tax credit form.
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a taxpayer to elect not to compute the amount of such taxpayer's earned income tax credit.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow businesses with average annual gross receipts of not more than $50,000 to recover their costs incurred in administrative or court proceedings.
Authorizes new alternative dispute resolution procedures for taxpayer disputes with the IRS.
Document 4: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to tax shelter activities to establish legal presumptions against the validity of transactions involving offshore secrecy jurisdictions.
Imposes reporting requirements for transactions involving a passive foreign investment company.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to require the Secretary of the Treasury to establish a program to allow taxpayers to download income tax forms that are populated with tax return information.
Establishes deadlines for reporting tax return information to the Secretary.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to require all employers to file wage withholding return information on magnetic media.
Allows a waiver of such requirement for employers demonstrating hardship or having five or fewer employees.
Document 7: Repeals the income tax, employment tax, and estate and gift tax.
Imposes a national sales tax on the use or consumption in the United States of taxable property or services.

topic.name: Federal Budget and Spending Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at managing federal budget allocations, spending, and financial oversight.

Document 1: Directs the Postal Service to issue a special stamp...
Requires Stamp sale proceeds to be used...
Document 2: This bill amends the Congressional Budget Act of 1974...
It also prohibits consideration of such provision in the House of Representatives.
Document 3: Amends the Jobs Accountability Act...
Terminates the requirement of the CBO and GAO comments...
Document 4: Requires the total budget resources for expenditures...
Declares that it shall be out of order in the House of Representatives...
Document 5: This bill establishes within the legislative branch a Mandatory Bureaucratic Realignment and Consolidation Commission...
Congress must consider the commission's proposal using specified expedited legislative procedures.
Document 6: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act...
Dedication of any such amounts so received for the sole purpose of debt reduction.
Document 7: Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act to require a remittance transfer provider...
Requires submission to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau of all fines imposed...

topic.name: Health Care Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on health care policies and benefits in the United States.

Document 1: Flexible Health Savings Act of 2007
allow up to $500 of unused health benefits to be carried forward
Document 2: Diaper Investment and Aid to Promote Economic Recovery Act
includes provision of diapers as covered child care assistance
Document 3: Adoption Information Act
require family planning service projects to provide a pamphlet containing a comprehensive list of adoption centers
Document 4: Keep Your Health Plan Act of 2013
permits health insurance issuers to continue offering certain coverage
Document 5: Flexible Spending Account Act
repeal the $2,500 limitation on salary reduction contributions
Document 6: Helping America Hear Act of 2007
requires coverage for hearing aids under federal employee health benefits plans
Document 7: Breast Cancer Patient Protect Act of 2009
requires health plans to ensure coverage for breast cancer treatment

topic.name: Health Disparities and Research
topic.description: Legislation aimed at addressing health disparities and promoting research in various medical conditions affecting minority and underserved populations.

Document 1: Medicare coverage for uninsured cancer patients
educational and outreach campaign for cancer testing
Document 2: ongoing research on diabetes in minority populations
programs to treat diabetes in minority populations
Document 3: study strategies for preventing synthetic drug use
national outreach campaign on synthetic recreational drugs
Document 4: grants for comprehensive HIV/AIDS services to racial and ethnic minorities
funding through Minority HIV/AIDS Initiatives
Document 5: establish National Institute of Autism Spectrum Disorders
conduct and support research on autism
Document 6: guidelines for nutrition education for health professionals
continuing medical education courses on disease prevention
Document 7: coordinate NIH paralysis research and rehabilitation activities
grants for clinical rehabilitation intervention protocols

topic.name: Government Accountability and Reporting
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at enhancing accountability and transparency in government operations through reporting requirements and oversight mechanisms.

Document 1: Directs the Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Communications and Information to conduct a competition to award grants
Requires the Comptroller General to conduct an audit of such competition.
Document 2: Requires annual reports of federal agency chief financial officers to include the cost of multilingual services
Requires the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to issue guidance for agency chief financial officers.
Document 3: Requires that the Secretary of Veterans Affairs submit reports in an electronic format.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to conduct a resource study and submit a report.
Requires the report to discuss the concerns of private landowners.
Document 5: Directs the Election Assistance Commission to publish a report containing best practice recommendations for redistricting.
Document 6: Reaffirms the authority of the Comptroller General to audit and evaluate financial transactions.
Requires elements of the intelligence community to cooperate with the Comptroller General.
Document 7: Requires the head of each federal agency to annually report to Congress on each covered contract.
Requires each report to be publicly available on the agency's website.

topic.name: U.S. Counterterrorism Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at addressing issues related to terrorism, national security, and immigration in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include grounds for loss of U.S. nationality related to foreign terrorist organizations.
Eliminates the grounds for loss of U.S. nationality by entering or serving in the armed forces of a foreign state.
Document 2: Prohibits assistance to a foreign country if it received an individual released from Guantanamo Bay who is suspected of returning to terrorist activities.
Document 3: Prohibits the use of DOD funds to transfer detainees held at Guantanamo Bay to countries that sponsor terrorism.
Document 5: Requires redeployment of U.S. Armed Forces from Iraq while allowing exceptions for counter-terrorism operations.
Document 6: Prohibits federal funds for the U.S. refugee admission of aliens from countries associated with terrorism.

topic.name: Transportation Safety and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures aimed at improving safety and regulations in the transportation sector.

Document 1: Seat Belts Save Lives Act of 2016
require a schoolbus to be equipped with a three-point safety belt
Document 2: Amends the SAFE Port Act
strengthen overall security of the international supply chain
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code
charitable tax deduction for contributions of qualified vehicles
Document 4: Surface Transportation Safety Act of 2011
ensure that positive protective measures are used to separate workers on highway construction projects from motorized traffic
Document 5: Driver Fatigue Prevention Act
apply its maximum hours requirements to over-the-road bus drivers
Document 6: Pipeline Modernization and Consumer Protection Act
accelerate the repair, rehabilitation, and replacement of gas piping or equipment that is leaking
Document 7: Bike to Work Act of 2014
include a bicycle sharing system as a mass transit facility

topic.name: Water Resource Management Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts related to the management, conservation, and infrastructure development of water resources across different regions in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the Platte River Recovery Implementation Program
modifications are consistent with program purposes
Document 2: limits the Secretary of the Interior's share of the costs associated with fish screens
to assist efforts in California to implement measures to avoid losses of juvenile anadromous fish
Document 3: Authorizes, ratifies, and confirms the Settlement Agreement of March 31, 2006
to provide grants and technical assistance to the Pueblo to construct, replace, or rehabilitate water infrastructure
Document 4: authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in design, planning, and construction of permanent facilities to reclaim and reuse water
Limits the federal share of project costs to 25%
Document 5: Deems requirements of the Endangered Species Act of 1973 relating to operations of the Central Valley Project
to implement habitat program under which each Secretary shall identify, prioritize, and implement key ecosystem restoration
Document 6: extend the authorization of appropriations through FY2015 for the planning, design, and construction of the Assiniboine and Sioux Rural Water System
and the Dry Prairie Rural Water System, in Montana
Document 7: Authorizes the Department of the Interior to carry out the projects entitled: the 'Dry-Redwater Regional Water Authority System'
Defines the service areas of such projects in North Dakota and Montana

topic.name: Air Quality Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative actions and amendments related to air quality standards and pollution control in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Clean Air Act to lengthen from 5 years to 10 years the intervals at which the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) is required to review and revise air quality criteria
eliminates the EPA's authority to review and revise criteria or promulgate new standards for ozone concentrations earlier or more frequently than required.
Document 2: Enacts into law the final rule entitled 'Federal Implementation Plans: Interstate Transport of Fine Particulate Matter and Ozone'
Requires compliance with such rule by: April 1, 2014, and annually thereafter for phase I sulfur dioxide and annual nitrogen oxide reductions and ozone season nitrogen oxide reductions.
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act (CAA) to consider as approved with respect to a taconite ore processing facility
Prohibits the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from promulgating, implementing, or enforcing any requirement pursuant to a federal implementation plan under the CAA.
Document 4: Amends the Clean Air Act to prohibit the issuance of permits under such Act for any carbon dioxide, nitrogen oxide, water vapor, or methane emissions resulting from biological processes associated with livestock production.
Document 5: requires the President to establish the Interagency Task Force on Short-Lived Climate Pollutant Mitigation
The bill amends the Clean Air Act to prohibit the manufacture of ...
Document 6: Prohibits the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) or the head of any other federal agency from regulating carbon dioxide emissions until the Secretary of Commerce certifies that China, India, and Russia have initiated measures that require carbon dioxide emission reductions.

topic.name: Civil Rights and Anti-Discrimination Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at prohibiting discrimination based on race, gender, sexual orientation, religion, and disability in housing, healthcare, and employment.

Document 1: Amends the Fair Housing Act, with respect to prohibited discrimination in housing sales and rentals
Adds to the list of prohibited actual or perceived bases sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, and source of income.
Document 2: Includes certain disclosures made by a Department of Defense (DOD) contract employee to his or her employer among those for which reprisal is prohibited.
Document 3: Prohibits discrimination on the basis of moral or religious objection in health insurance coverage
Creates a cause of action for the Attorney General or any person or entity adversely affected.
Document 4: Amends the Civil Rights Act of 1964 to require employers to make an affirmative effort to accommodate religious practices
Prohibits an employer's refusal to permit an employee's use of general leave to accommodate religious practices.
Document 5: Prohibits discrimination on the basis of sex, race, color, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, or disability status against any person in the United States under any health care service
Applies to any health care service or research program receiving federal financial assistance.
Document 6: Requires certain employers to permit each employee to earn at least one hour of paid sick time for every 30 hours worked
Prohibits an employer from interfering with an employee's exercise of such rights.
Document 7: Amends the Americans with Disabilities Act to prohibit civil action for discrimination based on structural barriers without prior written notice
Requires accommodation owners to outline improvements to remove barriers.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Legislation
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures related to U.S. immigration policies and procedures.

Document 1: prohibits the obligation of federal funds for the U.S. refugee admission of any alien: (1) whose country of nationality is Syria
Such prohibition shall terminate only upon the approval in the House of Representatives and in the Senate of a resolution of termination.
Document 2: This bill amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to direct the Department of Homeland Security to authorize employment for an asylum applicant
Employment authorization shall be: (1) for a period of one year, and (2) renewable for additional one-year periods.
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include within the H-2A nonimmigrant visa category
Provides for a three-year initial period of admission with additional three-year extensions.
Document 4: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to exempt certain returning H-2B aliens from annual numerical limitations.
Document 5: Sets forth apprehension procedures for immigration enforcement-related activities engaged in by the Department of Homeland Security
Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to: (1) require DHS detention facilities to implement procedures to ensure that child custody and family interests can be considered.
Document 6: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include in the definition of 'immediate relative' the child of a parent of a U.S. citizen
who is accompanying or following to join the immediate relative parent.
Document 7: Amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2008 to provide that the total number of aliens eligible for special immigrant status
Imposes a deadline of December 15, 2013, for applications for special immigrant status.

topic.name: Veteran and Military Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on benefits, recognition, and support for veterans and active military personnel.

Document 1: Eliminates specific dates on which the POW/MIA flag must be displayed
provides that such flag shall be displayed on all days on which the U.S. flag is displayed.
Document 2: Permits a veteran with any compensable service-connected disability to use commissary and exchange stores
on the same basis as a member of the Armed Forces entitled to retired or retainer pay.
Document 3: Requires that a member of a reserve component of the Armed Forces who was deployed for more than 179 days
be retained on active duty in the Armed Forces for 90 days after the end of the member's demobilization.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Defense to provide for a one-year program under which postal benefit vouchers are provided
for the benefit of members of the Armed Forces on active duty in Iraq or Afghanistan.
Document 5: Authorizes the governor of a state to proclaim that the U.S. flag shall be flown at half-staff
in the event of the death of a first responder working in such jurisdiction.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the military department concerned to award the Purple Heart to members of the Armed Forces
who were killed or wounded in the attack at Fort Hood, Texas.
Document 7: This bill authorizes the Department of the Army to award the Army Combat Action Badge
to a person who participated as a member of the Army in combat during which the person was personally engaged with the enemy.

topic.name: Tariff Suspension and Amendments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the temporary suspension of duties on various goods, particularly footwear and chemicals.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on sports footwear for women
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on 1-Hydroxy cyclohexyl phenyl ketone
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: extend the temporary suspension of duty on camel hair
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on certain women's footwear
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 5: suspend temporarily the duty on certain PCBTF with acid acceptor
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 6: temporarily provide for the duty-free treatment of certain leathered footwear for women
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to allow duty-free treatment for ethyl alcohol
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Federal Appropriations and Budgeting
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to federal funding and appropriations for various government programs and agencies.

Document 1: Amends the National Sea Grant College Program Act
Requires that sea grant colleges provide extension services
Document 2: Makes FY 2008 appropriations for the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers
Sets forth limitations upon reprogramming of funds
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations for FY2011-FY2015 for construction of vessels
Loan guarantees and commitments for the construction of new vessels
Document 4: Appropriates funds for FY2009 for the Department of Defense
Specifies restrictions and authorizations regarding the use of funds
Document 5: Authorizes appropriations for FY2011 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Specifies that the amounts authorized are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations
Document 6: Increases and extends through FY2010 the annual authorization of appropriations for Department of Veterans Affairs
Comprehensive service programs for homeless veterans
Document 7: Authorizes the Comptroller General to conduct reviews of any credit facility established by the Federal Reserve Board
Specifies the credit facilities to which this Act applies

topic.name: Communications and Spectrum Policy
topic.description: Legislation and regulations related to the management and allocation of communication spectrum and broadcasting rights.

Document 1: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require each satellite carrier providing secondary transmissions in the local market of a television broadcast station to carry, on request, the signals of all television broadcast stations located within any local market.
subject to existing broadcast signal retransmission consent provisions.
Document 2: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to biennially inventory each radio spectrum band from 300 megahertz to at least 6.5 gigahertz.
make the information available to the public on a searchable Internet website.
Document 3: Directs the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to initiate a proceeding not later than June 1, 2008, to reevaluate and reconfigure its band (spectrum) plans.
requiring the reconfiguration to include spectrum to be licensed for small geographic areas.
Document 4: Directs the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to ensure the deployment of a nationwide public safety interoperable broadband network in the 700 MHz band.
Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to increase the electromagnetic spectrum allocation for public safety services by 10 megahertz.
Document 5: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to allow retransmission of a television broadcast station's signals by a multichannel video programming distributor to a subscriber located in the station's adjacent underserved county.
Requires the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to revise regulations concerning network nonduplication, syndicated exclusivity, and sports blackout protection.
Document 6: Requires the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to direct providers of commercial mobile services and commercial mobile data services to permit their subscribers to unlock any type of wireless device used to access such services.
Prohibits this Act from being construed to alter the terms of any valid contract between a provider and a subscriber.
Document 7: Directs the Administrator of General Services (GSA) to install Wi-Fi hotspots in all publicly accessible federal buildings constructed after enactment of this Act.
allow for the installation of wireless neutral host systems by any eligible carriers upon request in all publicly accessible federal buildings.

topic.name: Election and Campaign Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reforming various aspects of election processes and campaign finance in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to redefine the term 'independent expenditure'.
Prescribes disclosure requirements for corporations, labor organizations, and certain other entities.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution.
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC.
Document 5: Requires in each election for federal office for inclusion on the ballot after the names of all candidates a separate line for 'None of the Above'.
Requires a new election within 30 days if 'None of the Above' receives the most votes.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 with respect to the use of campaign contributions.
Prohibition against the conversion to personal use of contributions accepted by candidates.

topic.name: Education Improvement Initiatives
topic.description: Legislative efforts aimed at enhancing educational outcomes and resources in K-12 schools, particularly in under-resourced areas.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to states, educational nonprofit organizations, or partnerships of such entities for their use in providing competitive subgrants to local educational agencies (LEAS) that have a high school graduation rate of no more than 60%.
Requires each LEA subgrantee to use the funds to implement a college-preparatory curriculum aligned with rigorous secondary school studies.
Document 2: This bill establishes a competitive demonstration grant program through which the Department of Education (ED) shall award matching funds to increase the number of school nurses in public elementary and secondary schools.
In awarding grant funds, ED shall give priority to high-need LEAs that demonstrate the greatest need for new or additional nursing services.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award planning grants and matching implementation grants to states to integrate engineering education into kindergarten through grade 12 (K-12) instruction and curricula.
Requires states to use implementation grants to develop or improve engineering teacher training programs.
Document 4: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to require states to develop environmental literacy plans for pre-kindergarten through grade 12.
Authorizes the Secretary to award competitive matching grants to partnerships that include an LEA for activities to improve and support environmental education.
Document 5: Prohibits the Department of Education from using funds to develop, implement, or evaluate any set of multistate academic content standards.
Prohibits the Secretary of Education from requiring states to adopt such standards or assessments as an eligibility requirement in an application for federal funds.
Document 6: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to include health education and physical education in the definition of 'core academic subjects.'
Document 7: Amends title II of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to authorize grants under the Teacher Quality Partnership grant program to be used for secondary school career and technical education teacher preparation programs.

Requires the career and technical education teacher preparation programs to be available to mid-career professionals or recent graduates of an IHE who have expertise in a program area in which there is a career and technical education teacher shortage.

topic.name: Wilderness Designation and Management
topic.description: The documents focus on the designation and management of federal lands as wilderness areas within the National Wilderness Preservation System.

Document 1: Designates specified federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Humboldt County, Nevada, as wilderness.
Sets forth provisions for the management of such land, including regarding livestock grazing, water rights, and fish and wildlife management.
Document 2: Designates identified federal land in Nevada managed by the Forest Service, to be known as the Wovoka Wilderness, as wilderness.
Sets forth provisions for the management of the Wovoka Wilderness, including regarding livestock grazing and wildfire.
Document 3: Expands the boundaries of the Thunder Bay National Marine Sanctuary and Underwater Preserve for the purpose of providing equal protection to underwater cultural resources.
Requires the resulting area to be managed as part of the National Marine Sanctuary System.
Document 4: Designates specified federal lands in Cherokee National Forest in Tennessee as wilderness.
As additions to the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Document 5: Designates specified federal land within Olympic National Park, Washington, as wilderness or potential wilderness.
Incorporates such land in the Olympic Wilderness.
Document 6: Designates specified Bureau of Land Management (BLM) and National Forest System lands in California as wilderness areas.
As components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Document 7: Prohibits road construction and road reconstruction in inventoried roadless areas of the National Forest System.
Specifies exceptions for natural resource restoration actions and public interest projects.

topic.name: Financial Regulatory Compliance
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of compliance and regulation within the financial sector, focusing on reporting requirements, the treatment of financial instruments, and institutional responsibilities.

1: failure to report a suspicious transaction is not a violation if certain conditions are met
evidence of bad faith constitutes a violation
2: treats stored value devices as monetary instruments
mandatory registration of stored value devices with the Secretary of the Treasury
3: directs SEC to revise regulations for qualified prepaid tuition programs
defines a qualified prepaid tuition program for investment purposes
4: requires securities registration applications to certify compliance with the Federal Election Campaign Act
periodic reports by issuers must include compliance statements
5: determining capital requirements for non-accrual loans
permits treating certain non-accrual loans as accrual loans under specific conditions
6: amends the Commodity Exchange Act to regulate credit-default swap trading
establishes a framework for financial regulatory reform
7: makes state credit unions eligible for Federal Home Loan Bank membership

topic.name: Border Security Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing security and enforcement along the United States' borders, particularly focusing on the prevention of illegal immigration and smuggling activities.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to submit to Congress a border protection strategy for the international land borders of the United States.
Prohibits construction of border fencing, physical barriers, roads, lighting, cameras, sensors, or other tactical infrastructure prior to 90 days after such border strategy's submission to Congress.
Document 2: Revises federal flight training requirements authorizing a flight instructor to provide training to an alien only upon receipt of information from the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security that the individual has been checked against the terrorist watchlist.
Prescribes the additional requirement that such training may occur only if the individual is determined to be a non-threat to aviation.
Document 3: Subject anyone who attempts to construct or finance construction of an unauthorized tunnel that crosses the international border to penalties.
Requires the Secretary of Homeland Security to submit an annual report describing cross-border tunnels along the U.S.-Mexico border discovered during the preceding fiscal year.
Document 4: Sets forth border security and enforcement provisions, including increases in Border Patrol and investigative personnel.
Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act and specified maritime law sections to revise alien smuggling provisions.
Document 5: Authorizes the Administrator of General Services to provide for the use by state or local governments of federal supply schedules for law enforcement and security equipment.
Document 6: Provides for continued National Guard support for border control activities along the U.S.-Mexico border until operational control has been achieved.
Authorizes the Secretary of Defense to provide funding to a state that submits a border control activities plan.
Document 7: Subject anyone who attempts to construct or finance construction of an unauthorized tunnel to penalties prescribed for such an offense.
Encourages the Secretary of Homeland Security to annually provide notification to landowners about federal laws related to illegal border tunnels.

topic.name: Regulatory Approvals for Medical Products
topic.description: This cluster focuses on the regulatory processes and legislative measures aimed at expediting the approval and safety of medical products and treatments.

Document 1: reciprocal marketing approval process
public health or unmet medical need for the product
Document 2: expedite the drug's development and review
breakthrough therapy determination
Document 5: consultation with external experts
review of drugs and biologic products for rare diseases
Document 6: market shortage for vital drugs
unreasonably excessive price for vital drugs
Document 4: adulterated meat products
contaminated with EHEC
Document 3: unlawful for any commercial user to manufacture, sell, or otherwise place in the stream of commerce any crib which was made before 1999
requires the CPSC to promulgate a consumer product safety rule

topic.name: Regulatory Acts for Safety and Accountability
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various regulatory acts aimed at enhancing safety, accountability, and ethical practices in different sectors such as animal welfare, labor rights, education, and government contracting.

Document 1: Amends the Animal Welfare Act to define a 'high volume retail breeder'...
Requires the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to promulgate requirements for the exercise of dogs at facilities owned or operated by a dealer.
Document 2: Requires each public or private company that produces or sells products in interstate commerce... to submit to annual audits of its supply chains by an independent auditor...
Determine if the chains are free of child labor and forced labor.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Education to establish a program to award grants... for initiating, expanding, or improving fire safety education programs.
Ensures maximum exposure to and increased awareness of fire safety by students.
Document 4: Directs the Postal Service to provide for the issuance and sale of a semipostal... to contribute towards the acquisition of works of art honoring female pioneers.
Document 5: Prohibits an office of the legislative branch from entering into a contract for goods or services... unless the contractor elects to participate in the E-Verify Program.
Document 6: Amends the Foreign Relations Authorization Act... to authorize the Secretary of State to award a local guard contract abroad under the diplomatic security program.
Document 7: Amends the Act authorizing the making, amending, or modification of federal contracts... to direct the Secretary of Defense... to report to Congress on all such actions taken by DOD.

topic.name: Small Business Program Enhancements
topic.description: Legislative amendments aimed at improving and expanding small business support programs within the Small Business Administration.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to direct each federal agency that is required to establish a small business innovation research (SBIR) program to issue research topic solicitations at least twice a year.
Establishes an Interagency SBIR/STTR Policy Committee.
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act to reduce state matching funds requirements for participation in the Federal and State Technology Partnership (FAST) and Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) programs.
Revises the SBA's Rural Outreach program to increase states' participation in the SBIR and STTR programs.
Document 3: Amends the Small Business Act to reauthorize for FY2008-FY2010 certain small business assistance programs of the Small Business Administration (SBA).
Establishes the Small Business Intermediary Lending Pilot Program under which the SBA Administrator provides loans to loan intermediaries.
Document 4: Requires the Department of Defense (DOD) to meet the annual goal outlined under the Small Business Act for participation in federal procurement contracts by small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans.
Document 5: Amends the Small Business Act and the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to raise, until January 1, 2011, the maximum amounts of loans for the benefit of small businesses under the following Small Business Administration (SBA) programs.
Suspends certain SBA loan fees during the period beginning on the date of enactment of this Act and ending on January 1, 2011.
Document 6: Amends the Public Works and Economic Development Act to include among the Act's goals encouraging distressed communities to support the formation of business incubators.
Authorizes the Secretary of Commerce to provide competitive grants for the development and implementation of feasibility studies and plans for the creation or expansion of business incubators.
Document 7: Amends the Small Business Act to raise from 23% to 26% the governmentwide prime contract award goal for participation by small business concerns.
Requires such procurement goals to be at least the average percentage of participation that occurred over the last three fiscal years.

topic.name: Housing Finance Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating various aspects of housing finance, including mortgage lending practices, credit scoring, and assistance programs for low-income families.

Document 1: amends the Federal National Mortgage Association Charter Act
authorize the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) to use credit scores in purchasing residential mortgages
Document 2: Amends the S.A.F.E. Mortgage Licensing Act of 2008
exempt from certain licensing and registration requirements any person who originates residential mortgage loans
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to make grants and loans
to carry out eligible housing stimulus activities
Document 4: Amends the United States Housing Act of 1937
prohibits the public housing agency from being required to review its income for any year
Document 5: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to prohibit the annual percentage rate of interest from exceeding 15%
subjects a creditor who violates this Act to a civil penalty
Document 6: Amends the National Housing Act with respect to the requirement that the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development
limits the use of any authority of the Secretary to borrow from the Treasury
Document 7: Amends federal bankruptcy law governing a Chapter 13 debtor
permits a Chapter 13 bankruptcy plan to modify the rights of claim holders with respect to a claim for a loan originated before the effective date of this Act

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Conveyance
topic.description: This topic encompasses the management, transfer, and withdrawal of federal lands for various purposes, including administrative jurisdiction changes and property disposal.

Document 1: directs the Secretary of Agriculture to release specified conditions on land for airport establishment
land conveyed to the state of Minnesota
Document 2: withdraws federal lands and interests in Pima and Santa Cruz Counties
withdraws all federally owned interests in BLM lands from entry and location
Document 3: transfers administrative jurisdiction of specified federal land
withdraws such land from the public domain
Document 4: sets forth requirements for expedited disposal of underutilized federal real property
requires federal agencies to maintain inventory controls for real property
Document 5: directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey specified lands for business and community development
requires approval of all parties for any such conveyance
Document 6: directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey National Forest System land for fire and rescue station
permits the District to use such parcel
Document 7: replaces the map relating to the Coastal Barrier Resources System
included in the set of maps referred to in the Coastal Barrier Resources Act
